rulings.Case Title: CASEY, PATRICIA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, APRIL 3, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint: As to prayer for damage (a), brought by defendant only as to Patricia Casey, defendant¿s motion is denied. Patricia Casey is not barred from making a claim for general damages according to proof and applicable law at trial. As to prayers for damage (b), (c), (d), (e), (g) and (h), defendant¿s motion is denied as moot as to Patricia Casey because she has dismissed those claims against defendant and defendant¿s motion is granted without leave to amend as to Catherine Grabinski and Jessica Casey. Catherine Grabinski and Jessica Casey have not dismissed any of their claims for damages against defendant. As wrongful death plaintiffs, they are barred from making a claim for these damages and thus leave to amend is denied because they cannot cure such defects. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CANDICE CLARK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Claim From Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Wrongful Death And Survival
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, APRIL 3, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s motion to strike punitive damages from plaintiffs¿ first amended complaint is denied. Plaintiffs¿ complaint states sufficient allegations to support a claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ROBARE VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, APRIL 3, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Specially appearing defendant National Cooperative Refinery Association¿s motion to quash service of summons is off calendar pursuant to the notice of removal filed March 15. = (503/TLJ)
